heroSubtitle: A master box, also called a master carton, is the corrugated outer shipping carton that carries coconut shisha charcoal through container freight, stacking, and warehouse handling — {{masterBoxSizes}} of charcoal net per box, plain kraft or printed with your brand.

atAGlance.heading: Master box at a glance
atAGlance.printValue: Plain kraft or printed with your brand
atAGlance.markingsValueTemplate: {{unNumber}} / Class {{unClass}} DG, origin, net weight, lot

construction.p1: A master box is the structural layer of the packaging system: it takes the stacking load in the container and the warehouse, so its construction — not the retail print — decides whether cartons arrive square. Ours is corrugated kraft board, produced in {{masterBoxWallOptions}}-wall variants.
construction.p2: Carton strength is specified by edge-crush test (ECT) rating, burst strength, flute profile, and corrugate weight; these decide how high cartons stack safely during 4–8 weeks at sea. The exact strength specification for your order's box size is confirmed with the quote.
construction.strengthSpecTemplate: Current spec: {{ect}}, burst {{burstKpa}}, flute {{fluteType}}, {{corrugateGsm}} gsm corrugate.

photos.alt: Closed branded coconut charcoal master box, three-quarter view
photos.caption: Printed master carton, ready for container loading.

photos.alt: Open coconut charcoal master carton showing packed inner boxes
photos.caption: The master box carries the stacking load; inner boxes carry the brand.

walls.p1: Single-wall corrugate is the standard for palletized loads and shorter routes; double-wall adds a second fluted layer for floor-stuffed containers, humid-season shipments, and high stacks — a heavier box that holds its edges under compression.
walls.p2: The double-wall upgrade is priced per ton as a white-label / custom-print add-on.

photos.alt: Single-wall versus double-wall corrugated cross-section comparison
photos.caption: Double wall adds a second fluted layer for stacking strength.

dims.p1: Net weight options per master box: {{masterBoxSizes}} of charcoal. Net weight counts the charcoal only; gross weight (charcoal plus packaging) is what freight calculations use, and is confirmed per configuration with the quote.
dims.p2: By net weight, {{holdsByWeightExample}}. Exact inner-box counts vary with the inner-box size and briquette shape — the per-SKU packing math lives on the product pages.

photos.alt: Coconut charcoal master box with measuring tape for scale
photos.caption: Outer dimensions are confirmed per box size with the quote.

printing.h2: Printing & branding on the master box
printing.p1: Master boxes ship plain kraft (neutral) or printed with your brand. Brown kraft mutes vivid colors, so full-color artwork is typically quoted with a white-top liner; which faces print is specified per design with the quote.
printing.p2: Or apply printed box labels as a lower-cost, fast-turnaround branding alternative to direct carton printing.

photos.alt: Full-color print detail on a coconut charcoal master carton
photos.caption: Full-color carton print; vivid color on kraft uses a white-top liner.

loadSecuring.p1: Cartons load two ways: floor-stuffed (maximum payload, manual unloading) or palletized on ISPM-15 heat-treated wood pallets (forklift handling, slightly lower payload). Load-securing options — strapping and edge protectors — are specified per order.

photos.alt: PP-strapped coconut charcoal master carton, strapping detail
photos.caption: Strapping keeps floor-stuffed stacks stable in transit.

photos.alt: Shrink-wrapped pallet of coconut charcoal master cartons
photos.caption: Palletized loading on ISPM-15 heat-treated pallets.

container.p1: A 20ft container loads {{containerNetKg}} kg of charcoal net — loose-loaded, that is {{boxesPerContainerExample}} master boxes. Palletized counts run lower and are quoted per order, since pallet footprint and box mix change the math.
container.hubFaqLinkLabel: Container-count FAQ on the packaging hub

photos.alt: Loose-loaded 20ft container stacked with coconut charcoal master boxes
photos.caption: Floor-stuffed 20ft container: {{containerNetKg}} kg net payload.

compliance.p1: Every export master box — neutral or branded — is printed with the {{unNumber}} marking and Class {{unClass}} dangerous-goods labels ({{imdgLabel}}), country of origin (Made in {{country}}), net weight, and a batch/lot code. Carriers check these markings before accepting the booking; missing marks strand cargo at the port.

photos.alt: UN 1361 dangerous goods and origin markings printed on a charcoal master carton
photos.caption: DG, origin, net-weight, and lot markings — mandatory on every export carton.

neutralVsBranded.p1: The neutral master box is plain kraft with compliance markings only — no printing surcharge, fastest turnaround. The branded box carries your artwork; custom sizes are quoted per design. If your boxes need to match retail inner boxes, brand both layers in one white-label run.
neutralVsBranded.p2: Want to check the carton quality before ordering? Request a sample master box through the proof and sample flow.

photos.alt: Neutral plain kraft coconut charcoal master box
photos.caption: Neutral carton: plain kraft, compliance markings only.

devilsAdvocate.tocLabel: A view from the other side
devilsAdvocate.h2: A View from the Other Side: Isn't a Neutral, Unbranded Carton a False Economy?
paragraphs: The hardest counter from an experienced importer runs like this: a plain kraft carton is a false economy. Retail buyers and distributors judge a brand partly by how its master cartons present on the warehouse floor, and a brown, label-only box reads as a no-name commodity that weakens shelf and re-order pull. Worse, a vendor who cannot publish an ECT rating, burst figure, or outer dimension is asking you to trust that the box is strong enough — on a Class 4.2 spontaneous-combustion cargo that sits four to eight weeks at sea. "Confirmed with the quote" is not a spec.

Part of this holds. The presentation point is fair for buyers selling into branded retail channels, where the master carton is part of how the brand shows up at the distributor. And the spec-transparency point is fair on its face: at present this page publishes no carton-strength number, because those fields are not yet filled in our source data. A buyer who needs the ECT figure to satisfy their own insurer or carrier is right to ask for it before booking, not after.

But neutral is neither unprotected nor unmarked. The protective layer is the carton's construction — corrugated kraft, single- or double-wall — which is identical whether the box is printed or plain; branding is print on the outside and changes nothing structural. And a neutral carton is not blank: every export box, neutral or branded alike, still carries the UN 1361 / Class 4.2 dangerous-goods marking, the country of origin, net weight, and a lot code, all of which carriers verify before accepting the booking. The compliance floor is the same. Because branding is decoupled from configuration, neutral versus branded is a free choice rather than a quality tier: a buyer who wants warehouse-floor presence brands the carton, while a buyer who wants the fastest, surcharge-free turnaround takes neutral — or applies printed box labels as a lower-cost branding step on a plain carton. None of these change how the box survives the container. The transparency objection is answered by quoting, not hiding: strength is specified per box size and wall type and confirmed with the order, and a buyer can request the ECT, burst, and dimension figures before booking, then request a physical sample master box through the proof-and-sample flow to verify carton quality first-hand rather than on a published sheet. In short, the presentation argument is a reason some buyers choose branded — which this factory fully supports — not a reason neutral is inferior protection or inferior compliance.

faq.heading: Master-box FAQ

items.q: What is a master box (master carton)?
items.a: A master box — also called a master carton — is the corrugated outer shipping carton that carries packed inner boxes or bulk charcoal through container freight, stacking, and warehouse handling. It is the structural layer of the packaging system: the inner box presents the product, the master box protects it.

items.q: What is the master box made of, and how strong is it?
items.a: Corrugated kraft board in {{masterBoxWallOptions}}-wall variants. Single wall is standard for palletized loads; double wall adds a second fluted layer for floor-stuffed containers and high stacks. Carton strength (ECT rating, burst strength, flute profile, corrugate gsm) is specified per box size and confirmed with the quote.

items.q: What master-box sizes and weights do you offer?
items.a: Net charcoal per box: {{masterBoxSizes}}. Outer dimensions and gross weight (charcoal plus packaging, the figure freight uses) are confirmed per configuration with the quote, since wall type and inner-box mix change both.

items.q: How many inner boxes does a master box hold?
items.a: By net weight, {{holdsByWeightExample}}. The exact count varies with the inner-box size and the briquette shape inside, so per-SKU packing math is confirmed on the product pages and with your quote.

items.q: Can the master box be printed with my brand?
items.a: Yes. Boxes ship plain kraft or printed with your artwork ({{acceptedArtworkFormats}}, CMYK; die-line provided). Vivid full-color on brown kraft is typically quoted with a white-top liner, and which faces print is specified per design. A lower-cost alternative: printed box labels applied to plain cartons.

items.q: Do you offer plain or neutral master boxes?
items.a: Yes — plain kraft with no printing surcharge and no extra minimum. Neutral is not blank: every export carton still carries the {{unNumber}} dangerous-goods marking, country of origin, net weight, and lot code, exactly like a branded box.

items.q: Are master boxes strapped, palletized, or loose-loaded?
items.a: Both loading modes are standard: floor-stuffed (maximum payload) or palletized on ISPM-15 heat-treated wood pallets (forklift handling). Strapping and edge protectors are specified per order as load-securing add-ons.

items.q: What markings are printed on the master box?
items.a: Every export box carries the {{unNumber}} marking with Class {{unClass}} dangerous-goods labels, country of origin (Made in {{country}}), net weight, and a batch/lot code. These are mandatory on neutral and branded cartons alike — carriers verify them before accepting the booking.

items.q: Can I order a custom master-box size, or request a sample?
items.a: Custom sizes are quoted per design through the white-label flow. A sample master box can be requested through the inquiry form or the samples page, alongside the digital proof ({{proofTimeDays}} working days) and physical pre-production sample.

faq.moreLine: Container counts and custom-print costs are answered on the packaging hub FAQ; order MOQ and payment terms on the general FAQ.
